title: Teaching and Mentoring
text:
Teaching Assistant
Precalculus, Calculus I-IV, Calculus for Biology and Medicine II-III, Introduction to Abstract Mathematics, Graduate Analysis II.

title: Teaching and Mentoring
text:
Mentorship
I have served as a mentor for 8 undergraduate reading projects on the following topics: C* Algebras, Special Relativity, The Fourier Transform in Circuit Analysis, The Cosmological Constant, Measure theory and the Dirac Delta function, Differential Geometry, Quantum Computing, and Knot Theory.

title: Notes — TNB Frame
text:
Code block (optional)
You can also include executable Python code cells if you have Jupyter installed:

import numpy as np
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt

t = np.linspace(-3, 3, 1000)
f = np.exp(-np.pi * t**2)
xi = np.fft.fftfreq(len(t), d=t[1]-t[0])
fhat = np.fft.fft(f) * (t[1]-t[0])

plt.plot(np.fft.fftshift(xi), np.abs(np.fft.fftshift(fhat)))
plt.xlabel(r"$\xi$")
plt.title("Magnitude spectrum")
plt.show()

title: Manim External
text:
This launches a full Jupyter environment in the cloud via Binder, where you can run the Manim TNB frame animation and edit the curve \(\vec{r}(t)\) directly in a notebook cell.






Note



First launch takes 1–2 minutes while Binder builds the environment. Subsequent launches are faster.





The notebook opens automatically
Edit r_sym and the t_start / t_end values in Cell 2
Click Run All (or press Shift+Enter through each cell)
The animation renders and plays inline



 🚀 Launch Manim Notebook 















Curve
r_sym




Default (Lissajous)
s.Matrix([2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)])


Helix
s.Matrix([cos(t), sin(t), 0.3*t])


Trefoil knot
s.Matrix([sin(t)+2*sin(2*t), cos(t)-2*cos(2*t), -sin(3*t)])


Torus knot
s.Matrix([(2+cos(3*t))*cos(2*t), (2+cos(3*t))*sin(2*t), sin(3*t)])

title: Home
text: Welcome to my site. Here you’ll find math notes, animations, and write-ups.

title: Home
text:
Quick example — inline LaTeX
The quadratic formula is \(x = \dfrac{-b \pm \sqrt{b^2 - 4ac}}{2a}\).
Euler’s identity: \(e^{i\pi} + 1 = 0\)

title: Home
text:
Display math
Maxwell’s equations in differential form:
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{E} = \frac{\rho}{\varepsilon_0}
\]
\[
\nabla \times \mathbf{B} = \mu_0 \mathbf{J} + \mu_0 \varepsilon_0 \frac{\partial \mathbf{E}}{\partial t}
\]
\[
\nabla \cdot \mathbf{B}=0
\] \[
\nabla \times \mathbf{E}=-\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{dt}
\]

text:
Embedding a GIF
Drop your Manim-rendered GIF into an images/ folder next to this file, then embed it like this:

With a caption and sizing:
![Double pendulum simulation](images/pendulum.gif){fig-align="center" width="70%"}
For .mp4 videos use the Quarto video shortcode:

title: TNB Frame Animation
text:
Edit r_sym in the cell below to define your parametric curve, then run all cells.
The animation will render and play inline.

from manim import *
import sympy as s
import numpy as np
from sympy import symbols, sqrt, lambdify, sin, cos, tan, exp, log, pi

t = symbols('t')


# ── Edit your curve here ──────────────────────────────────────────
r_sym = s.Matrix([2*cos(3*t), 2*sin(t), 2*cos(2*t)])

# t range for the animation
t_start = 0
t_end   = 2 * float(pi)
# ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────


%%manim -qm TNBFrame

class TNBFrame(ThreeDScene):
    def construct(self):
        def TNB_get(func):
            v_sym = func.diff(t)
            speed_sym = sqrt(v_sym.dot(v_sym))
            T_sym = v_sym / speed_sym
            N_sym = T_sym.diff(t) / sqrt(T_sym.diff(t).dot(T_sym.diff(t)))
            B_sym = T_sym.cross(N_sym)
            T = lambdify(t, T_sym, 'numpy')
            N = lambdify(t, N_sym, 'numpy')
            B = lambdify(t, B_sym, 'numpy')
            return T, N, B

        axes = ThreeDAxes(x_length=12, y_length=10, z_length=8)
        self.move_camera(phi=PI/3, theta=PI/4)
        self.add(axes)
        self.wait()

        r_temp = lambdify(t, r_sym, 'numpy')
        r_plot = lambda t: np.squeeze(r_temp(t))

        C  = ParametricFunction(r_plot, t_range=[t_start, t_end], color=RED)
        r_l = MathTex(r'\vec{r}(t)=', latex(r_sym) color=RED)
        T_l = MathTex(r'\vec{T}', color=GREEN)
        N_l = MathTex(r'\vec{N}', color=BLUE)
        B_l = MathTex(r'\vec{B}', color=PURPLE)
        TNB_l = VGroup(T_l, N_l, B_l)

        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(r_l.shift(3*UP + 3*RIGHT).scale(0.75))
        self.play(Create(C), run_time=5)
        self.wait()

        tracker = ValueTracker(t_start)
        T_f, N_f, B_f = TNB_get(r_sym)
        T = lambda t: np.squeeze(T_f(t))
        N = lambda t: np.squeeze(N_f(t))
        B = lambda t: np.squeeze(B_f(t))

        T_p = Vector(T(t_start), color=GREEN).shift(r_plot(t_start))
        T_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(
            Vector(T(tracker.get_value()), color=GREEN).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))
        N_p = Vector(N(t_start), color=BLUE).shift(r_plot(t_start))
        N_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(
            Vector(N(tracker.get_value()), color=BLUE).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))
        B_p = Vector(B(t_start), color=PURPLE).shift(r_plot(t_start))
        B_p.add_updater(lambda m: m.become(
            Vector(B(tracker.get_value()), color=PURPLE).shift(r_plot(tracker.get_value()))))

        self.add(VGroup(T_p, N_p, B_p))
        self.add_fixed_in_frame_mobjects(TNB_l.arrange(DOWN).next_to(t0, 2*RIGHT))
        self.wait()
        self.play(tracker.animate.set_value(t_end), run_time=10, rate_func=linear)
        self.wait()
